topic.what: Evaluation of Zuojin Pill — a two-herb formula of Coptis chinensis and Evodia rutaecarpa in a 6:1 ratio — for H. pylori-associated chronic atrophic gastritis in an animal model, assessing anti-inflammatory and gastroprotective effects.
topic.how: In vivo animal study using H. pylori-infected rat model of atrophic gastritis; Zuojin Pill administered orally; primary endpoints include gastric mucosal pathology (gland atrophy, intestinal metaplasia scores), inflammatory markers (NF-κB pathway, IL-1β, IL-8), and H. pylori colonisation density.
topic.why_matters: Chronic atrophic gastritis is a pre-neoplastic lesion on the Correa cascade; TCM formula Zuojin Pill combines heat-clearing (Coptis/berberine) with warm dispersing (Evodia/evodiamine) properties, representing a classical cold-hot combination strategy that may address both infection and mucosal deterioration.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Histological improvement in gastric gland atrophy and intestinal metaplasia
characteristics.value: Significant reduction in atrophy and metaplasia scores compared to model control
characteristics.vs_baseline: H. pylori-infected untreated atrophic gastritis model
characteristics.evidence: Haematoxylin-eosin and Alcian blue staining of gastric tissue sections
characteristics.context: Animal model; atrophy reversal in humans is harder to achieve and requires longer observation periods

characteristics.what_it_measures: Suppression of NF-κB-driven inflammatory signalling in gastric mucosa
characteristics.value: Decreased NF-κB p65 nuclear translocation, IL-1β and IL-8 protein levels vs. model group
characteristics.evidence: Western blot and ELISA on gastric tissue lysates
characteristics.context: NF-κB suppression is consistent with known berberine pharmacology

good_for: Research on berberine-evodiamine synergy in gastric mucosal protection
KB articles on cold-hot combination formulas as a TCM design principle
Researchers targeting pre-neoplastic gastric lesion reversal
not_for: Clinical prescribing without human trial evidence
Isolating the contribution of each herb without split-arm design
requires: Access to H. pylori animal model protocols
Understanding of TCM cold-hot (han-re) combination rationale

novelty: Demonstrates that a minimal two-herb formula with opposing thermal properties achieves gastroprotection in an atrophic gastritis model
Links NF-κB pathway suppression explicitly to the cold-hot herb ratio design principle
open_problems: Optimal Coptis:Evodia ratio for human atrophic gastritis not established
Long-term effect on intestinal metaplasia progression/regression in humans unknown
Interaction between Zuojin Pill and concurrent H. pylori eradication therapy not studied
